q: In Dataverse, what are plug-ins implemented as?
why: Plug-ins are .NET assemblies (classes implementing IPlugin) registered on Dataverse events.

q: Dataverse is described as 'metadata-driven and solution-aware'. What does that enable?
o: Customizations are tracked as components that move via solutions
Plug-ins run only in canvas apps
why: Being metadata-driven/solution-aware lets customizations be packaged and transported via solutions.

q: Decide whether each Dataverse development statement is true.

rows.s: Plug-ins can extend both model-driven apps and other clients that write to Dataverse.
The extensibility model exposes APIs for code to interact with Dataverse.
Dataverse cannot be extended with .NET code.

why: Dataverse extensibility supports .NET plug-ins/APIs affecting any write path to the platform.

q: Which is a benefit of Dataverse's extensibility model for developers?
o: Server-side logic that applies regardless of the client
It removes the need for security
why: Server-side extensions run for every client, enforcing logic consistently.

q: In the event framework, what flows through the pipeline for an operation like Create?
o: An event message with stages you can register steps on
why: Messages (Create/Update/Delete...) traverse pipeline stages where plug-in steps register.

q: Which pipeline stage runs AFTER the main database operation?
o: None; there is no post stage
why: Post-operation runs after the core operation; pre-validation/pre-operation run before it.

q: Decide whether each event-framework statement is true.

rows.s: Synchronous steps run immediately and can affect the operation's outcome.
Asynchronous steps run in the background via the system job queue.

why: Sync runs inline (can block/alter), async runs in background; pre-validation is earliest, not last.

q: You need to STOP an invalid Create before it is committed. Which execution mode/stage fits?
o: A synchronous pre-operation (or pre-validation) plug-in that throws
why: A synchronous pre-stage plug-in can throw to cancel the operation before commit.
